violations.text: Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information.(Dates of last used ) were lacking. The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black and pink residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit was taken out serviced and cleaned during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm.PIC made service call during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (500) parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (200-300) ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

(Grilled chicken breast), which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours.PIC voluntarily disposed of product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (PIC), (Grilled chicken ) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The door to the outside, located in the (kitchen ), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The (walls ) in the (basement and dining room booth(across from bar) ) area is in poor repair. Observed hole and damaged/missing drywall. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

Inspector observed ROP fish thawing with package still intact. PIC informed of proper procedures for thawing ROP seafood products. PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the (outside dumpster) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. List of certified instructors and application for state certification given. Info for certification to be sent to inspector at email given. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
Inspector observed an employee rinsing a hand towel in the handwashing sink.PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024
The restrooms are lacking handwashing signage. A sign or poster that notifies food employees to wash their hands must be provided at all sinks. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

Shellstock (steamer) located in (walk in cooler) did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty.PIC informed and tag was placed with shellstock. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024

Shellstock tags are not maintained for 90 days. Shellstock tags or labels are required to be maintained for 90 calendar days from the date the container is emptied. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC made service call during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice voluntarily disposed and unit was taken out of service to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024

Pasta, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours.PIC informed and product was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024
Inspector observed ROP fish thawing with package still intact. PIC informed and cut a slit in package. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (on top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement area. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2024
Inspector observed leakage at pvc pipes in basement. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the dumpster area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
